ModuleName: Introduction to Machine Learning for Business Analytics

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: A structured outline of the module's four main topic areas — ML foundations, supervised vs. unsupervised learning, common algorithms (linear regression, decision trees, k-means), and model evaluation — that instructors use to plan lessons and learners use to navigate what will be covered.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: A topic overview page listing the core subject areas covered in the module — including ML foundations, key techniques, and business applications — which learners use to understand the module's scope and instructors use as a curriculum reference.

Artifacts.Name: Introduction_to_Machine_generated.pptx
Artifacts.Description: A slide deck introducing machine learning in a business analytics context, walking learners through core ML concepts, key terminology, and the end-to-end ML workflow, intended for use as the primary presentation during lectures or self-paced study.

Artifacts.Name: Foundations of Machine Learning
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces the mathematical and statistical foundations underlying machine learning, covering core concepts and principles that learners need before studying specific algorithms or business applications.

Artifacts.Name: Supervised vs. Unsupervised Learning
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains the distinction between supervised and unsupervised learning, describing how the presence or absence of labeled training data shapes algorithm selection, data preparation, and analytical decisions.

Artifacts.Name: Common Machine Learning Algorithms
Artifacts.Description: This reading surveys commonly used machine learning algorithms, explaining how they identify patterns in historical data to generate predictions, classifications, or groupings, giving learners a practical reference for algorithm selection.

Artifacts.Name: Model Evaluation and Performance Metrics
Artifacts.Description: This reading covers model evaluation methods and performance metrics, explaining how to measure a model's effectiveness on unseen data and avoid pitfalls such as overfitting, serving as a guide for assessing model reliability.

Artifacts.Name: Machine Learning Applications in Business
Artifacts.Description: This reading explores real-world business applications of machine learning — including recommendation systems, fraud detection, and personalization — helping learners connect algorithmic concepts to practical organizational use cases.

Artifacts.Name: End-to-End Exploratory Analytics Project
Artifacts.Description: This reading walks learners through every stage of a complete analytics project, from defining a business problem to delivering results to stakeholders, providing a practical end-to-end framework that integrates all module concepts.
